stemHtml: Which of the following describes the Ice Water Content (IWC) of a cloud?

options.a: The IWC is a ratio of the liquid water to ice water within clouds and it increases with temperature.
options.b: The IWC is measured in g/m3  and it reaches maximum values in the upper levels of the core of convective systems.
options.c: IWC is defined as the cloud ice volume per unit mass of the atmosphere and the highest densities are found within Cirrus clouds.
options.d: As altitude increases within a convective cloud the IWC decreases, with the lowest values found in the anvil.
